source.title: The Cortico-Limbo-Thalamo-Cortical Circuits: An Update to the Original Papez Circuit of the Human Limbic System

confidence_rationale: Recent tractography-based review of the limbic system, explicitly updating the classical Papez circuit with modern diffusion MRI connectivity. Directly substantiates the reciprocal multi-pathway topology the 2026-04-16 synthesis relied on from Bubb 2017. Mapping fidelity is high for architectural claims (ARC-007 hippocampal map navigation and MECH-094 top-down tag delivery both require these pathways). Source quality is moderate -- review of imaging data, not mechanistic electrophysiology. Transfer risk from human tractography to the agent architecture is standard.
failure_signatures: Tractography cannot resolve the directional write-gating hypothesis; diffusion MRI shows structural connectivity but not which projections carry tag-delivery signals.
The review consolidates the limbic system as multi-circuit, which contradicts any REE design that tries to locate the hypothesis_tag in a single anatomical node.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The original Papez circuit (cingulate / entorhinal / parahippocampal / hippocampus / hypothalamus / thalamus) is anatomically incomplete; diffusion tractography reveals a richer cortico-limbo-thalamo-cortical architecture incorporating prefrontal/orbitofrontal cortex, septum, amygdala, and anterior temporal lobes as core components of the limbic network, with multiple reciprocal fibre pathways rather than a serial loop.
mapping.ree_translation: The anatomical substrate for MECH-094 top-down hypothesis_tag delivery to hippocampal encoding includes prefrontal/orbitofrontal input alongside cingulate input, consistent with Schnider 2003's OFC reality filter. The substrate for ARC-007 hippocampal map navigation is a multiply-connected network, not a single linear pathway -- severing any one pathway is predicted to produce graded rather than binary deficits.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Tractography establishes structural connectivity, not functional write-gating. The paper does not directly test whether any of the identified pathways mediates mode-specific encoding; that functional specificity must be inferred.
mapping.source_context: Human diffusion-weighted imaging review; healthy adult reference anatomy.

confidence_components.notes: Review, not primary data; high mapping fidelity because the claim is architectural; moderate transfer risk because human anatomy is the canonical target for REE biological grounding.
